meaning: adj.不能兑换的,不能变换的
examples: Exchange control restricts the right of holders of a currency to exchange it for other currencies. It thereby renders a currency inconvertible.
IMF, which knows an inconvertible currency when it sees one, pays them solemn respect.
The decoupling of work and reward, which inconvertible paper money does, causes a social, and spiritual, malady.
The Indian currency, the rupee, was inconvertible and high tariffs and import licensing prevented foreign goods reaching the market.
